text:
Once there was a small town with a secret. A secret kept by an old oak tree. In the heart of the forest stood a tree that had witnessed countless changes. Its bark was weathered with stories, and its gnarled branches had witnessed births and deaths.

Every autumn, villagers would decorate it with bright flowers, hoping their acts of love would bloom inside. But no magic stirred from its hollow trunk.

Then one chilly evening, a peculiar shadow danced beneath its branches. A young girl named Lily came to visit. With a whisper-light step, she climbed onto the ancient oak, her brown hair swishing back like a waterfall.

"Hello," she said softly. "You've watched me since I was a baby."

The oak tilted its head, offering a wise silence.

"You've seen every joy, every sorrow," she mused. "You've seen me fall and rise. Do you believe in love?"

The tree rustled gently. "Like the leaves in autumn? Many."
